channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Mexico presses case against Google over Gulf of America name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum accused Google on Monday of violating the country's sovereignty, leaving open the possibility of a lawsuit over the tech company labeling the Gulf of Mexico as the "Gulf of America" in its maps feature.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Trump, Musk say media trying to drive them apart
item.description: President Trump and Elon Musk, one of his top allies, argued that the mainstream media is trying to drive a wedge between them and stymie the president's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: DOGE says it has saved government $55B so far
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) in a post on its website says it has found $55 billion in savings through a combination of efforts, including a reduction in the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Apple’s celebrating the 2025 MLS season kicking off with a custom logo for each and every team
item.description: Apple is putting a fresh spin on the Apple TV logo. Well, actually, there are 30 fresh spins, one for each team in Major League Soccer ahead of the 30th season kicking off.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Ubisoft announces Rainbow Six Siege X, 'the biggest transformation' the tactical shooter has ever seen that will overhaul gameplay and graphics
item.description: Ubisoft has announced Rainbow Six Siege X, an upcoming update for the first-person tactical shooter that will see " major upgrades all around".
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft spies a new and worrying macOS malware strain
item.description: The XCSSET infostealer was updated after more than two years, and now comes with new persistence and obfuscation mechanisms.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump and EU Commission set course for confrontation on big tech
item.description: width="2193" height="1234" sizes="(max-width: 2193px) 100vw, 2193px">With the second Trump administration, very different cultures are once again clashing in the transatlantic relationship. rawf8 – shutterstock.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New iPhone SE 4 leak suggests it won't match the iPhone 16 in one key area
item.description: Apple is rumored to be putting its own 5G modem in the iPhone SE 4, which may lag behind the Snapdragon equivalent.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nintendo is set to discontinue Gold Points rewards a week before the Switch 2 Direct
item.description: In a baffling move, Nintendo will be discontinuing Gold Points rewards from eShop purchases in March.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk’s xAI launches Grok 3, expanding AI capabilities with deep search and reasoning
item.description: Elon Musk’s AI startup xAI has introduced Grok 3, the latest version of its chatbot model, which Musk describes as the most advanced AI system yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Come costruire un team di successo e vincere la battaglia per i talenti
item.description: Avere un team IT ad alte prestazioni è sicuramente uno dei principali obiettivi dei Chief Information Officer. Riuscirci, ovviamente, dipende dalle persone che costituiscono la squadra di lavoro, ma la capacità del CIO di capire le sue risorse e comunicare la strategia è ugualmente importante.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple may be launching a new iPhone this week. It could sport a radical redesign
item.description: If you’re on the cusp of buying a new iPhone, you might want to hold off until this Wednesday, February 19. That’s the day Apple CEO Tim Cook says the company will be launching “the newest member of the family.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: WordPress Foundation bid for greater trademark control halted, adding to more legal setbacks for CEO Matt Mullenweg
item.description: WordPress foundation requested to disclaim rights to Hosted WordPress and Managed WordPress, but it's not a denial for the trademarks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cultura y tecnología, palancas para accionar la transformación digital del sector público
item.description: Para que el sector gubernamental sea verdaderamente productivo, es fundamental priorizar tanto la cultura como la tecnología. Así lo destaca un nuevo informe de The Economist Impact respaldado por SAS.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Net neutrality under Trump? Not so neutral
item.description: Even before President Donald J. Trump returned to office last month, net neutrality took a punch to the jaw. On Jan. 2, the US Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit struck down the Federal Communications Commission’s (FCC) net neutrality rules. Oh well, it was nice while it lasted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 key features of Postman’s AI Agent Builder
item.description: The software landscape is shifting from passive business processes to dynamic, AI-driven workflows. AI agents—systems that interact with APIs, make decisions, and execute complex tasks—are at the forefront of this transformation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mac users beware: AI-powered malware threats are on the rise
item.description: Even Apple products are vulnerable to cybercriminals, and a new report suggests the owners of Mac products will have to be more vigilant this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inflatable tiny house with space for six is blowing up
item.description: An inflatable six-person tiny house from a firm called 2001 looks like a space station with its inflatable dome that is transportable and eco-friendly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A partnership between Jigsaw and this Kentucky city could be the future of civic engagement
item.description: Bowling Green, Kentucky, is known for being the city from which Corvettes roll off the production lines, and for Fruit of the Loom underwear, which is headquartered there.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Treasury watchdog office is auditing DOGE’s access to federal government’s payment system
item.description: The Treasury Department’s Office of Inspector General on Friday said it was launching an audit of the security controls for the federal government’s payment system, after Democratic senators raised red flags about the access provided to Trump aide Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency team
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The NBA's tech summit turns 25 this year, and its return to the Bay Area is a full-circle moment
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver had no idea 25 years ago that the Tech Summit would turn into much of anything, much less one of the hottest tickets at All-Star weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Buckle up for faster Python programs
item.description: In this edition of our biweekly Python newsletter: The next version of Python brings an all-new variety of speed boost. Python packages may soon pack better labeling for their ingredients. Cython 3.1 tees up new features for converting Python to C.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Avoiding the cloud migration graveyard
item.description: Fictional global retailer StyleHaven embarked on its cloud migration journey, eager to modernize its systems and improve its customers’ experience. StyleHaven opted to “lift and shift,” assuming its existing infrastructure would transfer to the cloud with few issues. The result? Chaos.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nvidia partners with cybersecurity vendors for real-time monitoring
item.description: Nvidia has partnered with leading cybersecurity firms to provide real-time security protection using its accelerator and networking hardware in combination with its AI software.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cisco doubles down on AI with new, updated certifications
item.description: Cisco this week furthered its commitment to help customers support and develop artificial intelligence systems by rolling out new certification and training courses aimed at teaching professionals everything from how to incorporate AI into specific roles to advanced networking design.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation
